Title: Multiplex CRISPR/Cas9-mediated metabolic engineering increases soya bean isoflavone content and resistance to soya bean mosaic virus

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Competing flavonol and anthocyanin branch of isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to dihydroflavonols, diverting substrate away from isoflavone formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to plant disease resistance and benefits human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR knockout of GmF3H1, together with GmF3H2 and GmFNSII-1, increased soybean isoflavone accumulation in leaves and seeds and enhanced resistance to soybean mosaic virus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmF3H1 was selected as a core competing-pathway enzyme target for multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 editing. 2. Its expression was significantly reduced in triple mutants. 3. Triple mutants showed increased naringenin and genistein/isoflavone accumulation in leaves and increased seed isoflavone content. 4. Increased isoflavones were associated with reduced SMV coat protein accumulation after infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; sequencing genotyping; qRT-PCR; metabolomics; isoflavone quantification; SMV infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression in seeds gradually decreased with seed development and maturity; reduced in mutant leaves and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SMV strain SC7 inoculation; seed developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most triple mutant lines showed no significant change in 100-grain weight, protein, or oil content; one line showed increased protein and decreased oil
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for multiplex knockout with other competing-branch genes to elevate soybean isoflavone content and improve SMV resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates GmF3H1 primarily in multiplex combination rather than as a single mutant

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Competing flavonol and anthocyanin branch of isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates flavanones such as naringenin to dihydroflavonols, diverting substrate away from isoflavone formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to plant disease resistance and benefits human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR knockout of GmF3H2, together with GmF3H1 and GmFNSII-1, increased soybean isoflavone accumulation in leaves and seeds and enhanced resistance to soybean mosaic virus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmF3H2 was selected as a core competing-pathway enzyme target for multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 editing. 2. Its expression was significantly reduced in triple mutants. 3. Triple mutants showed increased naringenin and genistein/isoflavone accumulation in leaves and increased seed isoflavone content. 4. Increased isoflavones were associated with reduced SMV coat protein accumulation after infection.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; sequencing genotyping; qRT-PCR; metabolomics; isoflavone quantification; SMV infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expression in seeds gradually decreased with seed development and maturity; reduced in mutant leaves and seeds
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SMV strain SC7 inoculation; seed developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most triple mutant lines showed no significant change in 100-grain weight, protein, or oil content; one line showed increased protein and decreased oil
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for multiplex knockout with other competing-branch genes to elevate soybean isoflavone content and improve SMV resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates GmF3H2 primarily in multiplex combination rather than as a single mutant

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Competing flavone branch of isoflavone biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanones such as naringenin and eriodictyol into flavones, diverting substrate away from isoflavone formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to plant disease resistance and benefits human health
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CRISPR knockout of GmFNSII-1, together with GmF3H1 and GmF3H2, increased soybean isoflavone accumulation in leaves and seeds and enhanced resistance to soybean mosaic virus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmFNSII-1 was selected as a core competing-pathway enzyme target for multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 editing. 2. Its expression was significantly reduced in triple mutants. 3. Triple mutants showed reduced flavone derivatives and increased naringenin, genistein, and downstream isoflavones. 4. Seed and leaf isoflavone contents were increased and SMV coat protein accumulation was decreased in homozygous triple mutants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Multiplex CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; sequencing genotyping; qRT-PCR; metabolomics; isoflavone quantification; SMV infection assay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in mutant leaves and seeds across developmental stages
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: SMV strain SC7 inoculation; seed developmental stage
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Most triple mutant lines showed no significant change in 100-grain weight, protein, or oil content; one line showed increased protein and decreased oil
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for multiplex knockout with F3H paralogs to increase soybean isoflavones and improve resistance to SMV
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper evaluates GmFNSII-1 primarily in multiplex combination rather than as a single mutant
